frameworks.name: subjective inflation
frameworks.one_line: peripheral/unattended percepts judged more reliable than matched-performance foveal/attended percepts

frameworks.name: higher-order theory of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: phenomenology constrained by cognitive access rather than overflowing it

frameworks.name: phenomenological overflow
frameworks.one_line: rich phenomenology said to exceed what cognitive access can report

core_question: Can subjective inflation explain rich-seeming visual phenomenology in the periphery without positing phenomenological overflow of cognitive access?
key_assumptions: visual phenomenology is constrained by cognitive access
objective performance can be equated across attended/foveal and unattended/peripheral conditions
subjective reliability ratings index phenomenological richness
key_findings: minimally attended or peripheral percepts are rated as more subjectively reliable than attended or foveal ones when objective performance is matched
this inflation pattern recurs across the reviewed Rich vs Sparse literature
interpretations: subjective inflation can account for apparently rich peripheral phenomenology without invoking overflow
phenomenology is constrained by cognitive access yet inflated above what sparse sensory content would predict
